programme.desc: Brendon and Priya want to explore Priyas bisexuality, but will her steamy hot tub and sensual time in the bedroom hit the spot? Chelsea and George disagree over how far they should take things.

programme.title: George Clarkes Old House, New Home
programme.desc: George is out and about with his sledgehammer, knocking holes in a fake Tudor ceiling in Leamington Spa, and moving doors in a farmhouse on the edge of the Peak District.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Travels with Mum and Dad
programme.desc: Matt Baker, his mum Janice and his dad Mike are hitting the road again for adventures in the north of England. First stop: the historic castle and distillery of Lindisfarne.

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun: Where Are They Now?
programme.desc: After finishing their gîte renovation, Kimmie and Matt hope for a busy summer of bookings. And on the Costa del Sol, Mike and Craigs cafe has been a runaway success. Can they stand the heat in the kitchen during their second season?

programme.title: Jasmine Harmans Renovation in the Sun
programme.desc: Its the final push, as the familys big summer bash is almost upon them. With guests about to arrive, can they gather momentum and complete everything in time?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps Dorset couple Andy and Barbara find their perfect holiday home in Benidorm. After a health scare for Andy theyve chosen to seize the moment and buy a bolt-hole in Spain.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Carpenter David from Suffolk wants a holiday home near the beach in Abruzzo on Italys east coast. Craig Rowe has lined up five great options for his £45,000 budget.

programme.title: Chateau DIY
programme.desc: Erin and JB race to light up their red salon before a drinks party. Anna and Edward rebuild their lakes sweeping stairway. Christina and Sassan transform their grand front gates.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It: Brilliant Builds
programme.desc: Kris and Emily wanted to add a fourth bedroom to their crammed three-bed home in Somersham, but Kirstie was convinced they already had enough space, and embarked on her biggest-ever declutter.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In and around Northampton, the first host is sports enthusiast James Cox. James is sure of a victory with a French/Italian themed menu.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the second day of the competition in Northampton where mum of three Donna Harris is cooking up three courses of comfort food in a bid to win a grand.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day three of the competition in Northampton and part-time actress Caroline Nash is hoping a menu based on her travels will dazzle her audience.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day four of the competition in the Northampton area, and hosting is party-loving Barb Wiles. Shes hoping to wow her guests with an Australian menu and to get them all to `buy into the Barb.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the last day of the competition in Northampton and finally taking centre stage is attention-loving businessman and keen cricketer, Duncan Fielding. Not one to take the middle road, Duncan has a menu based on the story of his life.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: 12-year-old Ruby-Rose is ready to move up from dog sitting to a dog of her own. Could Georgie the cheeky Yorkie be the one? And will ex-cabbie Danny give Bella the collie a lift?

programme.title: Worlds Most Secret Hotels
programme.desc: Exploring unique and surprising hotels throughout the world with innovative features and spectacular settings, from cabins dangling from clifftops to clandestine subterranean crash pads.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: Sir Robert, whos 93 years old, is rushed to St Georges with a low heart rate. Brad, whos crushed his finger at work, talks about what masculinity means to him and male role models.

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Critically ill dialysis patient Gillian desperately needs fluids, meaning the crew of East Anglian Air Ambulance needs to make a tough decision.

programme.title: Celebrity Gogglebox
programme.desc: Some of Britains best loved famous faces share their sharp, insightful, funny and emotional views on popular and topical TV shows. From Saturday night entertainment juggernauts to the weeks biggest news stories, celebs become opinionated viewers.

programme.desc: In a particularly active episode, Armando Iannucci performs a daring stunt, Kumail Nanjiani moves bowling pins with oven gloves, and Joanna Page unwisely attempts a handstand.

programme.title: Johnny Reno
programme.desc: Johnny Reno is a US Marshall who is bushwhacked by outlaws on his way to Stone Junction, Kansas. Joe Connors and his brother Ab mistakenly believe the lawman is after them and fire on Reno, and Reno captures Joe after Ab is killed in the gun battle. The two face an angry mob in a town where local Sheriff Hodges is in cahoots with the corrupt Mayor Jess Yates.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Rich and Ash are after a potentially armed and dangerous man whos suspected of smashing up a property, and Macca pursues a stolen Ford whose occupants leg it into a field.

programme.title: The Motorway Cops: Catching Britains Speeders

programme.title: Platoon 24/7
programme.desc: Nearing the end of training, 13 Platoon faces its biggest tests: a large-scale battle simulation and endurance competition, carrying 30 kilos of kit over 45 inhospitable kilometres.

programme.title: Police: Suspect No. 1
programme.desc: After a violent road rage incident, two suspects flee by motorcycle at dangerous speeds. Police soon discover one of the culprits is also wanted for another brutal attack. The pressure is on to make a watertight case to keep the suspects behind bars.

programme.title: The X-Files
programme.desc: Scully mysteriously appears in a Washington hospital, alive but in a coma, and Mulder must fight to keep her alive in order to find out what happened to her and who did it. Meanwhile, Scully fights her own personal battle as she decides whether to stay or go on to the next world.

programme.title: Scam City
programme.desc: Conor Woodman convinces a scammer in Istanbul to give him a first-hand look at the clever techniques he uses to fleece his tourist victims.

programme.title: Outback Truckers
programme.desc: An insight into this rarely seen world of high stakes, colourful characters and extreme conditions.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Lanterns only female driver Merrie is tasked with the removal of an abandoned lorry, but it is soon clear this will not be a routine job. As she takes a closer look, she discovers that the lorry is housing many a guilty secret.

programme.title: Trucking Hell
programme.desc: Rory risks life and limb to recovered a skip lorry from the M1. Jim has a happy reunion with his own truck. Chris has to find a way of separating two HGVs that have somehow become wedged together.

programme.desc: Two men in a van cause destruction during a 60-mile pursuit through in counties. The runaways ram multiple police cars before forcing Jon and Jules into a barrier at 70mph.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: A drug dealers car flashes up on the police radar, leading to a risky interception, and officers pursue a pair of knife-waving criminals in a stolen vehicle.

programme.desc: An urgent call leads to a multi-vehicle pursuit to rescue a child. PC Ian Cosgrove tracks down the suspect on the M6. In Cheshire, a vehicle with suspected links to organised crime has been seized.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: The Interceptors pursue a motor suspected to be involved in several car thefts, while the Operation Revive team raid a house that has been converted into a cannabis factory. Bomber tackles a suspected drink driver, who gives him the silent treatment.

programme.title: Police: Night Shift 999
programme.desc: In Gloucestershire, the night shift police race to catch burglars with the help of police dog Elmo, and a helicopter joins the search for a driver who crashed into a gas main and disappears into the night.

programme.title: The X-Files

programme.desc: Chris and the team are in pursuit of a stolen Mazda M3, and the driver disappears into an industrial area. Chris tracker dog Ronny has a twitchy nose and they hope this canine crime fighter will manage to track down the villain.

programme.desc: The interceptors make an early hour dash to catch a team of late night burglars and one of the suspects treats Liam to a face full of saliva. Jacko and Simon are in pursuit of a speeding driver who abandons the car and legs it.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: For the first time, a pursuit is filmed from the suspects perspective. Plus Liam and sniffer dog Louis help uncover thousands in cash and a £1/2 million of class-A drugs. Finally, a 999 call causes firearms officers to rush to Guisborough at 11pm.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: The traffic cops are alerted when a car is spotted by police cameras being driven with no insurance. PC Andy Swift stops a car after suspecting its not insured. The driver gets straight out and is very aggressive.

programme.title: Traffic Cops
programme.desc: Even though its only just after 10am, a woman is suspected of drink-driving and traffic cop Matt Copestake is called to investigate. Traffic cop Andy Swift is on the trail of a driver who has allegedly failed to stop after a collision.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: West Yorkshire is one of the UKs highest crime areas. With this proud region seeing its criminal activity levels spiralling, the Interceptors use every tool in their arsenal to deliver justice.

programme.title: Police Interceptors
programme.desc: Runaway drivers are convinced to stop by the threat of 50,000 volts. A drink driver resists arrest, earning a face full of incapacitating PAVA spray. A fresh-faced and uninsured driver tries to pass himself off as his 52-year-old dad.

programme.title: Police Interceptors

programme.title: Air Force One
programme.desc: High-altitude thriller in which the presidential aircraft, Air Force One, is hijacked by Russian communist radicals demanding the release of the Kazakhstani leader. With the First Lady and her family trapped on board, the hijackers threaten to kill one hostage every half-hour until their demands are met. What they dont know is that the president himself is hiding in the hold and formulating a plan to save his family.

programme.title: Mini Matinee

programme.title: Walter and Dude
programme.desc: Walter and Dude are looking for an item that blows air, turns around and cools you down. Is it a spinning top, a hairdryer, or an ice lolly? Join them as they discover its a fan!

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes

programme.desc: Pix and Leo make friends with a cute little duckling but the poor duckling cant find his family. Come see how Pix and Leo help out.

programme.title: My Robot and I
programme.desc: Kim, a curious girl, introduces her robot companion, Kit, to simple and fun activities. While she initially believes she is teaching him, she soon realises that Kit has lessons of his own, leading to a unique friendship.

programme.title: Walter and Dude
programme.desc: Walter and Dude look for an item that plays music and produces sound when blown on it. Join the two friends as they find out whether it is a whistle, a train, or a trumpet.

programme.title: Welcome to the Island
programme.desc: Coco, the wise coconut tree, Bucky, the curious rock, Lily, the little flower, and Blossom, the happy papaya tree, explore themes of fostering and building friendships while having fun on their small island.

programme.title: Hungry Henry
programme.desc: Henry searches for his favourite dish and reveals how different foods are prepared.

programme.title: What a Wonderful Day
programme.desc: Its afternoon! Join the fun and play in the playground! Watch the robin and lark as they clean their lovely nest! Dream on the grass and have a fine rest! Come along, feel free to be happy - you and me! Swing along and sing a nice song!

programme.title: Songs and Rhymes
programme.desc: When we meet somebody everywhere we go, we say hello. Ola, bonjour, hello - Hello has different names - in English, French or Spanish. Lets greet our friends from all around the world with a nice hello in their language.

programme.title: Giggle Wiggle
programme.desc: Moving and dancing and encouraging children to have fun by imitating the choreography they see on screen. A series of animals help keep children moving to the sound of catchy songs and rhymes.

programme.desc: Mr Snail learns a lot when he goes on his amazing adventures with his friends and neighbours.

programme.title: Gàrraidhean Mòra
programme.desc: Mairi Rodgers travels to the Royal Botanic Garden Edinburgh, where she meets the gardens most famous resident, New Reekie.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.sub-title: Air Feadh An t-Saoghail ----- Around The World
programme.desc: Meet learner Andrew Gourlay from Fife, and Calum Maclean is in Perthshire to explore the myths connected to one of Britains oldest trees.

programme.title: An La
programme.desc: Local, national and international news from BBC ALBA.

programme.title: Mas Fhior
programme.desc: Niall Iain Macdonald hosts a quiz show where two teams go head-to-head to win a cash prize. Niall Iain Dòmhnallach le farpais cheist ùr is dà sgioba a strì airson duais airgid.

programme.title: Chernobyl: Utopia na Smal
programme.desc: In 1970, the Soviet Union begins construction of the Chernobyl nuclear power plant and the nuclear city of Pripyat.

programme.title: Somhairle Spins
programme.desc: Somhairle and his university friends take on the infamous Five Ferries cycle route, with an entertaining mix of experience, resulting in tired legs and sore backsides!

programme.title: Miann na Maighdinn-mara
programme.desc: Kate keeps heading south and meets up with Liam Crouse, who takes her out for a trip around Lochboisdale.

programme.title: Samhradh air Sàl
programme.desc: Katie MacRishnidh fishes with Angus and talks about the role fishing plays in the life of the island community.

programme.title: Machair
programme.desc: Drama Gàidhlig stèidhichte ann an Leòdhas. Gaelic soap based on the Isle of Lewis.

programme.title: Alleluia
programme.desc: Iain MacKinnon presents from Coats in Paisley. Amongst the singers performing are Amy MacKay, Emma MacLeod and James Graham. Plus the choir Soisgeul conducted by Gareth Fuller. Dolina Matheson reflects on her beliefs with a selected reading and thought, spiritual poetry and landscapes in the company of Donald E Meek in Tiree and Chloe Steele performs a tune of worship on the pipes from Beauly Priory.

programme.desc: A new view from Scotland.

programme.title: Stan and Gran
programme.desc: Melanie wont go to her favourite beach because of a strange sound known as The Wizards Whistle! Is it a bird? Is it a kettle? Stan and Gran investigate!

programme.title: Beathaichean a Bocadaich
programme.desc: Tha Sophie agus a caraidean a bocadaich coltach ri each agus sioraf. Sophie and her friends dance like a horse and a giraffe.

programme.title: Meaban and Moo
programme.desc: Tha Linda a tadhal air Meaban is Moo gus dùbhlan a chur romhpa. An-diugh feumaidh iad rudan a tha dubh a lorg. Linda challenges Meaban and Moo to find black items.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha Meanbh is Beag son rudan a cheartachadh an dèidh dhaibh tubaist adbharachadh. Meanbh and Beag cause an accident which involves bubble flowers, the Sky-Moo and the Iron Chicken.

programme.title: Na Clangers
programme.desc: Tha Am Màidsear s Beag Chlangair san long-chiùil, ag iasgach san adhair. Am Màidsear and Beag Chlangair are in the music boat, fishing in the sky.

programme.title: S E Iasg a ThAnnam
programme.desc: A butterfly fish shows off his good looks, describes where he lives and explains why he is covered with bright colours. He also demonstrates how he keeps safe from predators at night.

programme.title: Stòiridh
programme.desc: Christine Primrose reads A Bheinn agus a Chreag.

programme.title: Clann Na Cruinne
programme.desc: Kerene loves dancing so much that she is going to start a dance group.

programme.title: Belle and Sebastian
programme.desc: Sebastien and Belle are inseparable friends. Hes a spirited little boy and shes a big white dog who had been abandoned. Exploring the valley and the Alpine peaks where they live, they come to the aid of people in difficulty.

programme.title: Seonaidh
programme.desc: Bitzer treats the Farmer to a restaurant-style dining experience, offering a delicious pie as the main course. However, when the pie goes missing, Bitzer makes the mistake of accepting Shaun and the flocks offer of help in the kitchen.

programme.title: Uaine
programme.desc: Calum visits two farms in Perthshire to find out about their working methods.

programme.title: Bogaisean is Gumbalan
programme.desc: When Willi causes the furnace fire to go out, leaving the gumbles freezing cold, he lures Smiggles to the gumbletree to try to enrage him enough to use his burning ears to relight the furnace.

programme.title: Bus Ghlaschú
programme.desc: The story of the Glasgow bus, a lifeline between Donegal and the Scottish city.

programme.title: SpeakGaelic
programme.desc: Joy Dunlop continues to guide people through further steps to speak Gaelic.

programme.title: Immigration Tracks
programme.desc: This week we experience the European flavour of Quebec City. And Anne follows the St Lawrence River to Montreal.

programme.title: Eilean Chanaigh
programme.desc: Canna lies off the coast of Skye in a cluster of islands known as the Small Isles. But with only 15 residents and difficult access to the mainland, Canna is a fragile community.

programme.title: Dàna: Scotlands Wild Side
programme.desc: The vast landscape of the Cairngorms attracts thousands each year. Coinneach Rankin sets out on a multi-day expedition to learn why its mountains are a paradise for climbers, runners and so many others.

programme.title: Leonardo da Vinci - Deisciobal an Eòlais
programme.desc: In Milan, Leonardo joins Duke Sforzas court, begins writing treatises, and paints a monumental fresco depicting the Last Supper.

programme.desc: Cuirm Òran Ùr aig Celtic Connections a comharrachadh òrain ùra Gàidhlig. Òran Ùr concert at Celtic Connections celebrating new Gaelic songs.

programme.desc: Martel meets an old friend in County Durham who appears determined not to learn from his previous mistakes. Martin is pleased with the area hes in, just maybe not the property. Tommy thinks a demolition derby might be in order in Kent.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles presents from Kingston in Surrey, with the reds and the blues led by experts Thomas Forrester and Gary Pe. Both teams are eager to make a profit. While the competition hots up, Eric gets on his bike at the Herne Hill Velodrome.

programme.desc: In Midsomer Norton, couple Polly and Callum hunt for high-end labels and vintage glass to raise funds for Macmillan. Polly finds a steal in designer footwear, Callum gambles on a dressing table.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: To launch a new spirit distilled on the cheese farm where he lives, Banjo is asked by farm owner Chris to design a rustic gin tasting bar using salvaged materials found on the farm. This design is deeply personal journey for Banjo, as he aims to give back to the people who welcomed him and his husband Ro into their community.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse visits Somerset and helps a couple find the perfect house to fulfil their dreams of a rural lifestyle.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Metal worker Diane hopes to swap her business suit for a welding jacket. Signwriter Sam is looking for a less physical career after being diagnosed with a rare spinal condition.

programme.title: The Finish Line
programme.desc: Roman Kemp, with sidekick Sarah Greene, hosts the quiz show where knowledge equals speed. Maria, Paul, Said, Max and Albert race in moving podiums across the studio floor to try to win £5,000.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Ravi is determined to keep his family together, Mark tries to help Lauren, and Elaine and Ian attend a gala.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan Carr kicks off the competition by tasking ten interior designers to show off their signature styles by transforming a beach hut on the Dorset coast. They must also demonstrate their ability to reuse and repurpose where they can. Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Jonathan Adler must decide who has created the Stand-Out Space and which designer will be the first to go home.

programme.title: Reporting Scotland

programme.title: Scotlands Home of the Year
programme.desc: The judges are in the central region and must choose between a Georgian listed property in Bridge of Allan, a passive house in Kinross-shire and a converted granary in Kippen.

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: Young crofters Noah and Bethany hope to build on their dads farming ambitions.

programme.title: Debate Night
programme.desc: Topical debate on the big issues affecting Scotland and beyond, with members of the public putting their questions to a panel of politicians and other public figures about the biggest stories and the things they care about the most.

programme.title: River City
programme.desc: Jodie is guilt-ridden when she learns the payback on Drew turned out to be a devious scam and tries to put things right. Meanwhile, Lydia braces herself to finally get checked out by Gillian, Nicole tests whether her theory has any weight, and Sams plans for a getaway are hijacked.

programme.title: Jules and Gregs Wild Swim
programme.desc: Jules and Greg brave the icy, deep waters of Scotlands Highland lochs.

programme.title: Scotcast
programme.desc: Conversation about Scotlands biggest news stories, hosted by Martin Geissler.

programme.title: Scotlands Greatest Escape
programme.desc: Judith Ralston and Grado are out and about exploring Ayrshire and its islands. Starting with a cycle around Millport and a stay in some luxury eco cabins, the pair head on to the Isle of Arran to visit the UKs first employee-owned resort. Back on the mainland, they get to grips with some Olympic stones and spend the night in a 14th-century castle.

programme.title: The Catch-Up

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve heads to the incredible Loango National Park on the western coast of Gabon and home to some awesome Deadly predators. First up, Steve heads out onto the waterways in search of the small but deadly dwarf crocodile.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Setting sail to the magical island of Bali, Steve and the team are out to find the elusive ocean sunfish. These deep ocean giants feed on poisonous jellyfish and ascend to the shallows for a spa session at a cleaner fish station.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: The five teams bid farewell to Europe and head into Asia, racing toward the remote riverside town of Halfeti, where the last to arrive will be eliminated from the competition.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Olivia, a gregarious, high-achieving 22-year-old, Charles, a thoughtful 27-year-old international student from China, and Nirvali, a sensitive young lady who has never dated, navigate the world of dating.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Olivias first ever date ends with a question mark. Dianne is ready to find love after a major change in her life.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Stacey heads to Bedfordshire to the private site where 23-year-old Chantelle lives with her husband, Swayley. Chantelle prides herself in keeping with the old Gypsy values her granny Rita taught her and sharing them on social media.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: In Britain the shoplifting is at an all-time high, incidents of violence and abuse against shop staff have skyrocketed, and a third of Brits have admitted to stealing through the self-checkouts. To understand whats causing this high-street epidemic, Stacey is meeting the people behind the crime and spending time on the shop floor with the staff who deal with shoplifting every day. In Manchester city centre, beauty and cosmetics chain Bodycare has one of its biggest and most shoplifted stores.

programme.title: The Royal Hotel
programme.desc: American backpackers Liv and Hanna take a job in a remote Australian pub for some extra money and are confronted by unruly locals and a situation that spirals out of their control.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Brazils favelas are considered extremely dangerous, making them one of the worlds most deadly places to grow up. As a result of poverty and a lack of government and police control, many young men here resort to drug dealing and gang crime under the control of the drug cartels. In other neighbourhoods, heavily armed criminal militia groups control day-to-day life with an iron grip, extorting residents - fail to pay or cross them, and the consequences can be deadly.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: South Africa has for years battled the scourge of rhino poaching, fuelled by insatiable demand for their horns in Asia. Ashley embarks on a mission to find out who is behind these brutal acts, discovering how the challenges young men face growing up in deprived rural communities can lead to illegal poaching. Hanging out with men from KwaZulu-Natal - or Zululand - he earns their trust and joins them on a legal hunt for bushmeat in order to understand what day-to-day life is like here.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley visits Marseille to meet police, street dealers and hitmen all caught up in bloody drug wars that are ravaging Frances second-largest city.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve starts his South African adventure in one of the weirdest places hes ever gone looking for animals - a power station, in the dark! Hes staking the location out for a little big cat that has so far eluded the Deadly 60 - the serval.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and his trusty team head to the frozen north of Norway. He follows the tracks of Europes largest cat, the elusive lynx, up into the hills in the hope of seeing one, and gets a little nervous when he meets some in captivity. Arctic foxes and musk oxen are also on the menu in this deadly snowy episode.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters
programme.desc: Alan Carr kicks off the competition by tasking ten interior designers to show off their signature styles by transforming a beach hut on the Dorset coast. They must also demonstrate their ability to reuse and repurpose where they can. Michelle Ogundehin and guest judge Jonathan Adler must decide who has created the Stand-Out Space and which designer will be the first to go home.

programme.title: The Apprentice
programme.desc: Its the final, and after 12 tough weeks, the last two candidates standing must go head-to-head in a battle to win Lord Sugars investment. In the boardroom, Lord Sugar decides who will become his next business partner.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Colombia has become synonymous with one major illegal export, cocaine, but now theres another equally lucrative product thats funding the powerful and deadly organised crime groups that control much of rural Colombia: gold.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Sweden has been a European hotspot for gang-related shootings and bombings for several years. But recently, the violence has shifted beyond low-income, vulnerable, urban areas. Trouble is expanding throughout the country.

programme.title: Ashley Cain: Into the Danger Zone
programme.desc: Ashley heads to the Philippines to find out if joining a gang is the only way to survive in the slum districts of the capital, Manila. Ashley explores how gang life has changed in the Philippines and what factors still drive young men to join them.

programme.desc: Khalil never remembers his dates names, so he calls them all baby girl to skirt around it. Obsessed with looking good, he checks himself out in the reflection of a spoon every 20 minutes on dinner dates.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Dee encourages a friend to say `snow, and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of snow - cold snow, falling snow, twinkly snow and yummy snow. Theres sparkly snow and sledging snow, and Dee and Penguin have fun throwing snow!

programme.title: Lu and the Bally Bunch

programme.title: Twirlywoos

Plus, the Action Song gets everyone moving about before some calm time with bubbles and the Goodbye Song.

programme.title: Monster Makes
programme.desc: Rebecca is keen to go on a travelling adventure and invites Murphy to come. Murphys time machine takes them to the dinosaur age, but when it stops working, they need to get making. Firstly, they create a paper plate dinosaur, followed by some colourful water blob creatures made by the Mini Monsters. Then, Rebecca gets a call on the Magic Monster Mirror from her aunt Antarctic, who makes herself some snow-building buddies to help rescue her snowman.

programme.title: Vegesaurs
programme.desc: Ginger and the Peas help a stubborn Baby Bokchoydactyl build its nest all by itself.

programme.title: Bluey

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: The Squirrels must walk back to the Clubhouse to get Duggee a bandage for his sore foot. Walking is so exhausting. Will they ever make it back. Not until the Squirrels meet a few of their friends on their way.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Howdy diddly diddly do. This song is all about all the different things that can be learnt with Duggee to earn badges. From singing to painting, dancing, playing and making friends too.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk to a meadow to put vegetable cuttings in the composter. On the way they fly like butterflies, wiggle like bees, look inside an apple and even meet a fence-eating tree!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: When Henry and his family go on a trip to a nearby pond, its fishing nets at the ready as they try their hand at a spot of shrimping. Its not easy as the tiny shrimp are good at hiding. Luckily, Henry comes up with a plan.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Naomis mum has taught her a very special way to make a special Senegalese seafood dish called mafe fish. Naomi prepares all the ingredients for her friends, including her one very special vegetable, savoy cabbage. When they taste the savoy cabbage for the first time, Naomis friends dont like it, so lets hope she can change their minds once it is cooked.

programme.title: Do You Know?
programme.desc: Maddie is finding out about different gates that open and close. She visits a canal lock, to find out how canal boats travel up and down hill.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Dan the border collie is a search and rescue dog who works with his owner Jamie. His super nose follows scents and smells so they can rescue people who need their help. A local artist is painting the view of Woofton from a hill. But when the wind gets too strong, he gets lost on his way down. Dan and Jamie are called to try to find him. Dan finds the artists scent, leading him to his painting.

programme.title: Steve and Aneeshwar Go Wild

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Mrs Hobbss cat, George, gets locked inside Mrs Hobbss house, giving Waffle a chance to prove himself to Mrs Hobbs. Can he rescue George?

programme.title: Maddie and Triggs

programme.title: Hey Duggee

programme.title: Ishq Vishk
programme.desc: It is about Rajiv and Payal who are friends since childhood. While Payal is in love with Rajiv, it takes him a bit longer to return her feelings.

programme.title: Mujrim
programme.desc: A man comes back home and tries to help his poor family after serving his term in jail for murdering his uncle, but all his efforts go in vain because of his reputation.

programme.title: Izzatdaar
programme.desc: Brahm Dutt, a rich steel magnate, is falsely convicted for the murder of his best friend. In prison, he finds out that his son-in-law Indrajeet has conspired against him and vows to avenge himself.

programme.title: Love Ke Liye Kuch Bhi Karega
programme.desc: A greedy man plans to have his wife kidnapped by two friends who are also in need of some quick cash.

programme.title: Love Aaj Kal
programme.desc: Jai and Meera have had a good run as a couple, but work calls Meera to New Delhi, while Jai wants to move to San Francisco. Although the two love one another, their ambitions take precedence over their passion and amicably they decide to part ways. Veer Singh, a prosperous Sikh who runs the cafe at which Jai and Meera call it a day, finds the whole arrangement thoroughly unromantic and odd.

programme.title: De De Pyaar De
programme.desc: A 50-year-old single father faces disapproval from his family and his ex-wife when he falls in love with a 26-year-old woman.

programme.title: Gabbar Is Back
programme.desc: Gabbar creates a vigilante military network to eliminate corrupt officials. As pressure mounts on the police to find out who is responsible for the killings, a special investigator is summoned to track them down.

programme.title: Ishqiya
programme.desc: While on the run, Kalujaan and Babban fall for a kidnappers seductive widow. The three plan to commit a crime unaware of the fate that awaits them.

They take in the breath-taking views with a ride on a mountain rollercoaster, before exercising their vocal chords when they try the Swiss tradition of yodeling. programme.sub-title: The One With the Baby Shower

programme.title: Everybody Loves Raymond

programme.desc: Rachel throws a surprise party for Emily so she can invite Joshua; Phoebe develops a craving for meat.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With the Free Porn
programme.desc: Ross acts on Monicas advice and tells Emily that he loves her just before her departure for London. Unfortunately Emilys immediate response isnt exactly reassuring. Elsewhere, Phoebe receives some rather surprising news about her pregnancy, while Chandler and Joey are glued to their TV for fear of losing the free pornography channel they are suddenly receiving.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With Rachels New Dress

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One With All the Haste
programme.desc: Ross tries to keep Emily from returning to London, and Monica and Rachel attempt to win back their apartment.

programme.title: Friends
programme.sub-title: The One with All the Wedding Dresses
programme.desc: Distraught over Rosss engagement, Rachel suggests to Joshua that he marry her, even though theyve only been on four dates. Meanwhile, Monica and Phoebe pick up Emilys wedding dress from a bridal shop, but Monica tries it on and loves it so much she wont take it off. Phoebe also buys one for herself.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Rachel and Ross reminisce separately about when they dated each other, and the friends get invitations to Rosss wedding, but not all of them are going.

programme.sub-title: The One With the Worst Best Man Ever

programme.sub-title: The One With All the Kissing

programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Gold Rush: Mine Rescue With Freddy and Juan
programme.desc: A familiar miner in Alaska desperately needs 150 ounces of gold to save their operation. But when Juan learns that their landowners mine is halfway to $1M in the hole, he and Gold Rush legend Dave Turin face their first-ever double rescue.

programme.title: Tanked

programme.title: Americas Backyard Gold
programme.desc: The former Idaho Territory of Idaho and Montana is still rich with gold; Dave shows where and how everyday miners think outside the box to get it.

programme.title: Americas Backyard Gold
programme.desc: Dave Turin shows you the small-time miners bringing in gold hard cash from Montanas hard rock. When a city slicker asks him to assess an old family mine, he goes on a mission and brings cool 21st-century tech to determine its riches.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A couple plans to build a unique outdoor living space in Colorado by using donkeys to get materials to the top of their steep build site. When their plans go haywire, however, they are left scrambling for solutions to finish the project.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike and Elvis are determined to celebrate a dying breed, which is the iconic V8 engine. Theyve chosen a classic sports car in order to do it, being the Mercedes SL55.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.desc: Mike finally gets his hands on an Astra GTE. With spare parts hard to come by, the boys are determined to make a profit from this lengthy project.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Henry and Fuzz meet Tim, a specialist bike restorer with a passion for all things British. Will they persuade him to part with a rare Triumph soft top?

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Motorcycle fanatic Henry Cole travels around the country with his mechanic mate Fuzz Townshend nosing around peoples sheds and garages in the hope of finding some automotive bargains.

programme.title: Richard Hammonds Workshop
programme.desc: Some of the precious classic cars and motorbikes go to auction with no reserve. Meanwhile, Neil and Anthony get to work on a mystery vehicle from the 1930s.

programme.title: Roadworthy Rescues
programme.desc: Derek enlists Steve Dulcich to help put the muscle back into a derelict Dodge Coronet. But with a completely seized drivetrain, the guys will have to replace everything on this car if they hope to make the 500-mile road trip back to Nashville.

programme.title: Garage Rehab
programme.desc: In Tampa, Florida, Richard and his team toss a lifeline to a custom motorcycle shop drowning in debt, clutter and broken equipment. Re-imagining the garage as a three part shop, Richard puts the business on track to becoming its own unique brand.

programme.title: Ant Anstead: Born Mechanic
programme.desc: Master mechanic Ant Anstead restores and sells classic cars with a hope to turn some profit to help fund the renovation of the 500-year-old farmhouse he bought for his mum and dad.

programme.title: James May and The Dull Mens Club
programme.desc: James devises a solution for applying sun cream solo. Plus, he has a plucky plan to deal with noisy neighbours and plays records on a pottery wheel.

programme.title: Salvage Hunters: Classic Cars
programme.desc: Salvage aficionado, Drew Pritchard teams up with Turbo Pickers Paul Cowland for an epic motoring mission. They scour the UK, buying, restoring and selling classic cars.

programme.title: Mike Brewer: Born Dealer
programme.desc: Mike takes the family on a trip down memory lane. He finds new cars for his dad and sister and helps Richard Hammonds mum sell her MX5 for charity.

programme.title: Wheeler Dealers
programme.sub-title: This Horse Needs To Be Put Out of Its Misery
programme.desc: Mike decides to pay it forward and help out a young member of the team by fixing up his 4-liter V6 2007 Mustang. Ant and Mike soon find out that just because this young man works with cars doesnt mean hes taken all Mikes advice to heart.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Motorcycle fanatic Henry Cole travels around the country with his mechanic mate Fuzz Townshend nosing around peoples sheds and garages in the hope of finding some automotive bargains.

programme.title: Shed and Buried
programme.desc: Bike guru Henry Cole and ace-mechanic Sam Lovegrove are in Cornwall, looking for something to buy, restore and sell on for a handsome profit. At a windswept hilltop farm they find cars, tractors and a very rare German scooter.

programme.desc: Shawn gets his first taste of the shifting seabed; Ken tries a new perspective to find new ground; Kris and Emily each try out greenhorn divers to boost their productivity, but the plan grinds to a halt for one of them.

programme.desc: Lin McAdam is a sharpshooter arriving in Dodge City, where a competition for marksmen has as its prize a priceless and perfect Winchester repeating rifle. McAdam wins the rifle but it is stolen by the runner-up, his rival Dutch Henry Brown. Determined to regain the rifle, McAdam sets off in pursuit of the thief, but his quest turns into an almost epic voyage through American myth and history.

programme.title: Von Ryans Express
programme.desc: World War II story about Allied prisoners who stage a mass breakout from an Italian POW camp, commandeer a train and head towards the Swiss border. As the Italian war effort collapses, the escapees are aided by their erstwhile captors, but when the Germans catch on, they set out to halt the escape with an armoured train and aerial attack.

programme.desc: When a divorced guys ex wife re marries someone way more uptight, he re-enters her life and wreaks havoc.

programme.title: Jennifers Body
programme.desc: In a bid to further their careers, indie band Low Shoulder made a pact with the Devil: one that requires the soul of a virgin. They kidnap and kill cheerleader Jennifer, but unfortunately she was no virgin and rises again to take her retribution.

programme.desc: Shula is the first child taken to a travelling witch camp, where she is told that should she cut the ribbon and attempt to escape, she will be cursed and transformed into a goat.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Rick and Chalky enjoy a Turkish barbecue in Londons East End. After contributing lamb kofta to the fare, Rick moves on to Hackney Marshes, where he meets various allotment holders and joins them for Sunday lunch.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes: Another Helping
programme.desc: Samantha Petters family-run organic cobnut orchard near Sevenoaks in Kent provides vital ingredients for sprats with a cobnut pesto. At Leigh on Sea, Rick samples the local cockles and jellied eels from Rawlings.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond is preparing for a party by making a tasty lineup of low-carb appetisers. Her menu includes sausage stuffed mushrooms, make-ahead courgette roll ups, baked brie a la Ree and jalapeno cheese crisps.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree makes a variety of sweet festive treats! They include cinnamon roll cookies, red velvet crinkle cookies, and no-bake cornflake wreaths.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals
programme.desc: The average price of the recipes in this episode is just £1.55 per portion. Jamie cooks up a glorious mothership roast side of salmon with all the trimmings. Then, then turns his leftover salmon into an incredible salmon filo pie.

programme.title: Jamies Money Saving Meals

programme.desc: Inas got weeknight dinners covered, pro-style! Recipes include roasted salmon tacos, cider roasted pork tenderloin and five-minute lemon capellini.

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: `The Great British Bake Off makes its debut on Channel 4 as the series returns for an eighth season with 12 budding bakers competing to be crowned the champion. Established judge Paul Hollywood is joined by food connoisseur Prue Leith, while seasoned entertainers Sandi Toksvig and Noel Fielding take on the role of being the shows hosts. Despite the change in personnel, the key Bake Off traditions remain intact, as the contestants try to impress the panel with a fine selection of beautifully-crafted and delicious cakes, tarts, breads and more.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from the Northern Ireland region. With two chefs remaining, the must cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world-class cooking will do. Just one chef can make it through to the National Finals to represent Northern Ireland.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan kick things off with their starters. Johnnie makes foie gras ice cream, cherry ravioli and iberico ham; Aiden offers black cherry and foie gras terrine with palm sugar mousse; and Simons entry is grilled salad, truffle custard, cheese foam and cobnut crisp.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan present their respective fish courses. Johnnie concocts a Recreation of the Sea; Aiden makes beetroot poached salmon, caviar, razor clam, citrus and fennel salad; and Simon serves up lobster with pickled beetroot, sweet apple and cuckoo flower.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan are challenged to cook their mains and Marcus Wareing judges the dishes.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Johnnie Mountain and Aiden Byrne along with newcomer Simon Rogan present their respective desserts. Its the final chance for the chefs to impress veteran Marcus Wareing as he decides who will go through to cook for the judges.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Chefs compete for the chance to cook at a glittering Olympic banquet. The North West finalists go head-to-head as they prepare a lavish meal for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In the battle of the London and South East chefs newcomers Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their starter menus. Will it be Phil with spring salad with goats milk puree, pickled asparagus and quails eggs; Graham with pickled rabbit, wild garlic quinoa and frozen rabbit liver parfait; or Marcus with iced lambs liver parfait, malt loaf, fingerling limes and rose geranium?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Newcomers Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their fish courses. Will it be Phil with a tasting of Cornish mackerel with oysters, mussels, winkles and samphire; Marcus with pollock, peas, coconut and elderflower; or Graham with octopus, blood orange, feta cheese, squid ink tuile and black olive salt?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness cook their main courses, but only one chef will take gold. Will it be Phil with roast loin of lamb with pie and mash, carrots, nettles and mint; Graham with slow cooked marsh lamb breast, rasher and spiced rissole; or Marcus with blade of beef cooked in hay, tendons and beetroot?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Phil Howard, Graham Garrett and Marcus McGuinness present their dessert dishes here. Will it be Phil with rhubarb and custard souffle, Marcus with asparagus, goats curd and black olive, or Graham with lemon meringue? Veteran Jason Atherton decides who goes through to the judges and who is going home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from the London and South East region. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do. The chefs task is to create a menu which captures the Olympic spirit.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the battle of the Wales chefs: returning contenders Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry. All are determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. Stephen Terry cooks `The Opening Ceremony, Richard Davies makes ravioli of quail, parfait of foie gras, roasted salsify and Madeira jelly, and James Sommerin cooks sage cream, onion, chicken and Welsh `Brie truffle toast.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry are all determined to knock out their rivals in the race to the banquet. They cook their fish course, but which one will prevail? Will it be Stephen Terry with `The Fish and Shellfish Medley, Richard Davies with compression of vine tomato, poached lobster and hand dived scallop, or James Sommerin with lobster and Iberico ham, spiced butter and broccoli?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry cook their main course, but only one chef will take gold. Will it be Stephen Terry with `The Bunny Pentathlon, Richard Davies with slow cooked rib of beef, beetroot ketchup, bone marrow and braised shallot, or James Sommerin with mangalitsa pork, carrot, liquorice and leek?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Richard Davies, James Sommerin and Stephen Terry cook their dessert, but who will impress? Stephen Terry with `Bronze, Silver or Gold?, Richard Davies with passion fruit cream and gel with bitter chocolate sorbet or James Sommerin with raspberry and lemon cheesecake? `Great British Menu veteran Angela Hartnett decides who goes through to the judges on Friday and who is going home.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: It is decision time for the chefs from Wales. The two remaining chefs cook their entire menus for judges Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: South West contenders Paul Ainsworth and Nathan Outlaw, plus newcomer Simon Hulstone, kick things off with their starters, but which one is going to be first off the blocks? Will it be Paul with his `Breakfast of Champions; Nathan with hogs pudding with seaweed, potato terrine and mushroom ketchup; or Simon Hulstone with track and field - beetroot and goats cheese?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their fish course, but which one but which one will prevail? Will it be Paul Ainsworth with monkfish `Two Showings; Nathan Outlaw with mackerel, horseradish and oyster sauce; or Simon Hulstone with `The Swimming Pool?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their main course, but which chef will take gold? Will it be Paul Ainsworth with `Wishful Chicken; Nathan Outlaw with duck, barbecue monkfish, rosemary, samphire and asparagus; or Simon Hulstone with lamb with salted toffee pea mousse, shallot puree and asparagus?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs final chance to impress the veteran with their dessert dishes. Will it be Paul Ainsworth with `Then and Now; Nathan Outlaw with elderflower and lemon tart, with strawberry sorbet and meringues; or Simon Hulstone with `Closing Ceremony?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time on Great British Menu for the chefs from the South West region. One chef left the competition on the previous day and only two remain to cook their entire menus for the `Great British Menu judges, Prue Leith, Matthew Fort and Oliver Peyton. The judges arent easily pleased and only world class cooking will do.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The 2012 competition enters its final week. The winners of the regional heats compete for a chance for their dish to be on the final banquet menu at a prestigious event hosted by Sir Steve Redgrave with a glittering guest list of British Olympians. Here, the finalists cook their starters, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu judges and veteran judge Richard Corrigan.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The eight winners of the regional heats prepare to cook their fish dishes, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu judges and veteran judge Marcus Wareing. Before they get into the kitchen, they must first learn whether they will all be cooking.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: On day three, the chosen finalists cook their main courses, which will be tasted and scored by the `Great British Menu judges and veteran judge Tom Kerridge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its desserts day in the finals, and the last chance for the competing chefs to impress the judges and veteran Angela Hartnett. After a hard-fought week, the end of this programme will see the judges finally reveal the winning four dishes which will make up this years `Great British Menu.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: This final programme follows the successful chefs in the run-up to the Olympic banquet at the Old Royal Naval College in Greenwich, as they make the painstaking preparations for what is one of the most important meals they will ever cook. The four winning chefs prepare an outstanding four-course menu for a truly Olympian guest list.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The spotlight is on Scotland. Returning contenders Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway are all determined to get one of their dishes to the banquet. Will a witty take on spiced ham score highly, or will a warm chicken salad with a twist or a rabbit terrine come out on top? Veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE will be judging.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning contenders Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway cook their fish dishes. Will a traditional Scottish soup, a chowder or a risky no-cook mackerel dish impress veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The competition heats up as the three returning Scottish contenders, Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway, cook their main courses. An African inspired goat tagine, a beef dish inspired by a childhood cartoon and a duck dish with a comedy twist are dished up for veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish returning contenders Michael Smith, Tony Singh and Mark Greenaway have just one more chance to impress veteran judge Angela Hartnett MBE and win their place in front of the judges. Will a comedy custard pie, a sophisticated chocolate dessert or doughnuts inspired by a Scottish tradition come out on top?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Only one chef can make it through to the national finals to represent Scotland and cook at a special banquet at the Royal Albert Hall.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The curtain raises on the competition in the North West. How will Aiden Byrnes dish entitled `Mad as a Box of Frogs go down with veteran Phil Howard? Will he trounce Mary-Ellen McTagues crab starter or will Chris Hollands `Oriental style beef broth with a twis score more highly with Phil?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North West chefs cook their fish courses. Will Aiden Byrnes `The Culinary Joke - a take on a classic prawn cocktail - raise a laugh, or will Mary-Ellen McTagues dish of dover sole or Chris Hollands `Gone Fishing salmon dish prove winners?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs cook their main course. Will Aiden Byrnes witty beef dish entitled `Long Horn Beef with Noodle Pot prove a winner, or will Mary-Ellen McTagues goat dish called `Our Kid raise a smile? Perhaps Chris Holland will score highly with his dish called `A Tasting of Pork.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the chefs last chance to impress before the final two are chosen to face the `Great British Menu judging panel. Which dessert will help get them through? Will it be Aiden Byrnes pineapple dish called `The Sweet Burger, Mary-Ellen McTagues take on a trifle called `Chamomile Lawn or Chris Hollands dish called `Going Bananas?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its decision time for the chefs from the North West. One chef has already left the competition and only two remain. The judges are joined by celebrity guest judge writer, presenter and performer Rowland Rivron.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: In central England, three culinary heavyweights return to the `Great British Menu kitchen. Two Michelin star chef Daniel Clifford creates a dish called `A Trip to the Chippy, where nothing is as it first appears. That is also the theme for Michelin star chef Richard Bainbridges starter, `banana split, while Will Holland offers up goats cheese mousse, balsamic jelly and gingerbread.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Here, the chefs try to impress with their fish courses. Daniel Clifford puts up a creative dish called `Catch of the Day. Does Richard Bainbridges dish titled `Tongue in Cheek live up to its comedy name? Will judge Marcus Wareing find the comedy value in Will Hollands coconut poached brill, crab, celeriac and tomato essence?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England cook their main courses. Daniel Cliffords main has the same title as Richard Bainbridges fish course, `Tongue in Cheek, but it is a wildly different plate of food; Richard Bainbridge presents guinea fowl in a beef bladder and calls the dish `Which Came First: the Chicken or the Egg; Will Holland offers roast longhorn beef rib eye, bread and bone marrow pudding and parsley paste.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England cook their main courses. Daniel Cliffords main has the same title as Richard Bainbridges fish course, `Tongue in Cheek, but it is a wildly different plate of food; Richard Bainbridge presents guinea fowl in a beef bladder and calls the dish `Which Came First: the Chicken or the Egg; Will Holland offers roast longhorn beef rib eye, bread and bone marrow pudding and parsley paste.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The chefs in central England have one last chance to impress Marcus Wareing before the final two are chosen to face the judging panel. Will Daniel Cliffords `Going Out with a Bang be good enough? Has Richard Bainbridge dropped the comedy ball with his coffee and chocolate dessert called `Time to Reflect? Or will an unusual dish of parsnip banoffee cake, chocolate tart and parsnip ice cream get Will Holland through?

programme.title: Battles Won and Lost
programme.desc: The battles of World War II increase in sheer size of the forces involved as well as the power of their weapons; a look inside Germanys strategies for Operation Barbarossa, as they attempt to invade the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Battles Won and Lost
programme.desc: A wide variety of strategies are needed during World War II, as battles are fought in every continent in many different landscapes; the English are cut-off at Dunkirk following unstoppable Nazis tearing through Western Europe.

programme.title: Battles Won and Lost
programme.desc: Battles are almost always about gaining territory, and advancing the front line to seize the port, the city, the oil field, or another objective; in the bombed remains of Stalingrad, Germany, soldiers are sentenced to death or held captive.

programme.title: Battles Won and Lost
programme.desc: New tactics are born in every war, to try to influence the course of battles. Campaigns are waged against non-military targets to ruin morale; long battles rage throughout the war, from Guadalcanal in the Pacific, to the brutal siege at Leningrad.

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The Emancipation Proclamation ends slavery and gives blacks the freedom to fight; on July 4, 1863, the day after Picketts Charge at Gettysburg, the Confederates surrender Vicksburg, Miss., to Gen. Grant.

programme.title: Civil War: The Untold Story
programme.desc: The Confederate Army of Northern Virginia succeeds at splitting the Union Army in two during the Battle of Chickamauga; Gen. William Rosecrans is replaced by Gen. Ulysses S. Grant, who plans an offensive.

programme.title: The Rise of Napoleon Bonaparte A Military Prodigy
programme.desc: Napoleon Bonaparte, born in 1769 in Corsica, came from minor nobility and claimed Roman heritage.

programme.title: Secrets and Mysteries of Christopher Columbus
programme.desc: Investigating the true origins of Christopher Columbus through historical records and testimony from experts and Columbus living relatives.

programme.title: Battles Won and Lost
programme.desc: World war involves many actions, large and small. Forces, climate, geography, and problems of supply must all be managed; Japan gathers naval strength for an attack on Midway Island as they attempt to lure the United States into a trap.

programme.title: History Of Rome Part 1
programme.desc: In 49 BC, Caesar crossed the Rubicon, sparking Romes transformation through myth and history.

programme.title: History Of Rome Part 2
programme.desc: Lives of the Caesars (121 AD) gives 12 bios of Romes rulers from the early Empire.

programme.title: History Of Rome Part 3
programme.desc: Explore the 3rd Century crisis, the rise of Constantine, and the ultimate fall of the Western Roman Empire.

programme.title: The Mystery Of The Roman Skulls
programme.desc: Engineers digging for Crossrail have made a chilling discovery: dozens of skulls under Londons Liverpool Street station. Its clear the skulls are from Roman London, but nothing like this has been found before.

programme.title: Egypt: New Discoveries, Ancient Mysteries
programme.desc: The truth about the pharaoh who amended Egyptian religion to monotheism.

programme.title: Ancient Giants: Legends of Anakims and Nephilim
programme.desc: Anthropologists may have discovered an ancient 7-14 foot tall giant human subgroup.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher discovers what life was really like for ordinary Egyptians 3,500 years ago, during the height of the incredible civilisation of ancient Egypt. Joann explores how people lived during this time, from the tightly packed houses and villages of the common people, to the clothes they wore and the food they ate.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher reveals a strange and mysterious world: the ancient Egyptian afterlife. To them waking life was just a dress rehearsal. Joann clambers into rarely visited tombs, explores a treasure trove of long-buried objects and examines spectacular mummies to discover just why the Egyptians spent a fortune preparing for death.

programme.title: Maya: Ancient Metropolis
programme.desc: Many mysteries remain surrounding the establishment of the Mayan city in Chichen Itzá. How was it constructed?

programme.title: Maya: Ancient Metropolis
programme.desc: Experts explore the ancient Mayan city of Tikal and ask why it was abandoned despite its dominance.

programme.title: Maya: Ancient Metropolis
programme.desc: Teotihuacan was discovered by Aztecs in the 14th century. But who built such magnificent structures as these?

programme.title: History Of Rome Part 1
programme.desc: In 49 BC, Caesar crossed the Rubicon, sparking Romes transformation through myth and history.

programme.title: History Of Rome Part 2

programme.title: History Of Rome Part 3

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Mafia And Britain
programme.desc: Ross Kemp investigates and exposes Britains surprising and deep-rooted links with the mafia, dating as far back as the 1920s.

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes With Billy Hayes
programme.desc: Correctional officer Kay Beeman feigns being a hostage to facilitate her inmate boyfriends escape. Their recapture would take months.

programme.title: Instruments of Death
programme.sub-title: The Wars of the Roses: The Battle of Towton 1461
programme.desc: Find out how the longbow, crossbow, falchion and hand cannon turned Towton into the bloodiest battle ever fought on English soil.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice discovers things at Norwich Castle Museum which highlight facts about Tudor life, Anglo-Saxon childbirth rituals, and the Pompeii of the Bronze Age.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice tells the story of Norman England by studying the history of Winchester, Britains most Norman city. Alice attempts to storm a castle in chain mail, investigates the origins of modern surgery and tucks in to an appetising eel pie.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: In Norwich, the UKs most Tudor city, Professor Alice Roberts learns about religious intolerance, uncovers the Norwich FC canarys origins and experiences humiliating Tudor justice.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Shipwreck Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross Kemp dives down on shipwrecks to uncover hidden stories, and recover treasure. He dives on warships, experimental submarines, early slaver ships and Napoleonic frigates, searching for clues and artefacts that reveal the story behind every wreck.

programme.title: Cracking the Code
programme.sub-title: The Code That Won the Great War
programme.desc: Uncover some of the worlds most renowned encryptions, revealing how they were decoded, the minds who cracked them and the secrets they masked.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice discovers things at Norwich Castle Museum which highlight facts about Tudor life, Anglo-Saxon childbirth rituals, and the Pompeii of the Bronze Age.

programme.title: Instruments of Death
programme.sub-title: The Norman Invasion: The Battle of Hastings 1066
programme.desc: Karl Ude-Martinez demonstrates how Danish battleaxes, Saxon shields and Norman lances played their part in the Battle of Hastings.

programme.title: Instruments of Death
programme.desc: The Somme campaign resulted in loss of life on a terrible scale. Karl Ude-Martinez looks at some of the weapons used, including machine guns and poison gas.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in Lincoln, getting to grips with life in medieval England. She visits the magnificent cathedral that was once the tallest building in the world and re-lives the battle at Lincoln Castle.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice is in London, as she attempts to make sense of the world-changing events during the short but eventful Restoration period. She learns the violent and vengeful truth behind Charles IIs carefully crafted public image.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson hikes 45 miles through the Weald in Kent and the Downs of East Sussex to discover the areas rich and surprising Tudor heritage. At the impressively preserved Penshurst Place, author Philippa Gregory helps Tony relish the fate of the Grand Duke of Buckingham at the hands of the young Henry VIII. Tony finishes in Lewes, where he relives one of the more brutal monastic dissolutions.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series Tony takes on a tough four-day trek through the Kintail region of the west Scottish Highlands to discover the story of the Jacobite uprisings of the early 1700s. On three occasions, Highland armies, assisted by the French and the Spanish, attempted to overthrow the King and put a Stuart back on the throne.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Viking Camp And Iron Age Gold
programme.desc: A look at the camp from which Vikings invaded Britain, and groundbreaking evidence that the world-famous Avebury stone circle isnt just a sacred site but a place where people lived and worked. In Staffordshire, the oldest Iron Age gold in Britain is unearthed.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: A look at the biggest collection of Roman writing tablets in Britain, giving insight into what Roman London was really like. Off the coast of Kent, a dive into the English Channel reveals the biggest marine excavation since the Mary Rose, an 18th-century East India Company ship packed with silver.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross heads to Portsmouth and the Mary Rose Museum and takes up an opportunity he cant turn down, to be one of the few divers allowed on the site of Britains most famous wreck, the Mary Rose.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Deep Sea Treasure Hunter
programme.desc: Ross hops round the coast from Portsmouth to Plymouth, then on to the Gulf of Suez. A bout of hypercapnia leaves Ross shaken after the dive of a 17th century shipwreck.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: After seizing power in 1933, Adolf Hitler began systematically transforming a peaceful German population into a ruthless war machine. Experts investigate the innovative methods used by the Nazis to indoctrinate and control the will of its citizens.

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.sub-title: The Secrets Of The Devils Bible
programme.desc: "The Codex Gigas," also known as the "Devils Bible," is a giant medieval tome that has evoked fear for centuries and believed to contain a malevolent power. Experts investigate its legendary secrets and uncover the mystery behind its author.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on his most curious walk yet, across the ancient and turbulent kingdom of Northumbria. In the seventh century, at the height of the Dark Ages, the rule of Northumbria stretched from Edinburgh to York.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson travels along the wonderful seascapes of the North Norfolk coast and through one of the least developed spots in the country.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice discovers things at Norwich Castle Museum which highlight facts about Tudor life, Anglo-Saxon childbirth rituals, and the Pompeii of the Bronze Age.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A house in Chesterton in Staffordshire has a smelly problem and needs a total overhaul. The new owner of a semi in Bestwood in Nottingham also has a lot to do, while the new owner of a flat in Strawberry Hill has less than a week to sort out her auction buy before she jets off to Germany.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A husband and wife duo add their design skills to a house in Croydon in Surrey, while a seasoned developer creates a high-end house in multiple occupation in Coventry and a first time developer also does a great job with a house full of period features in Nutall in Nottinghamshire.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts meets one of two young brothers busy building a property portfolio in Bolsover, Derbyshire, while Dion Dublin checks out a three-bedroom semi in Wolverhampton, and Lucy Alexander is impressed by plans to convert a two-bedroom, fire-damaged terraced house in Londons Plaistow into a smart five-bedroom property.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion Dublin has a hair-raising experience at a barbers in Rochdale, Lancashire, while Martin Roberts looks round a great bungalow in Alsager, Cheshire, and Lucy Alexander sees all the potential in a piece of land in Eccles in Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts visits a house in Otterton, Devon, which is given a stunning transformation to match the views from the windows, Lucy Alexander looks round a small one-bedroom flat in Tottenham, London, and Dion Dublin peers into a building in Alvaston, Derby, which is in a dangerous state of disrepair.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a one-bedroom flat in Catford, London, a large house near the River Mersey and a semi-detached property in Alvaston in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Victorian terrace in Londons Greenwich, two flats in Chatham in Kent and a riverside inn near Great Yarmouth in Norfolk are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners and hear plans for their renovation projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom house in Borrowash in Derbyshire, another three-bedder in West Bromwich in the Midlands and a quirky four-bedroom house on the Isle of Sheppey are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Pegswood in Northumberland, a terrace in Swadlincote in Derbyshire and a former shop in Chatham in Kent are given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a young couple expecting a baby who have to weigh up whether to live in their auction buy in Sydenham in London while Martin talks to a man and his father in law who have bought their first property together in Trentham near Stoke and Dion meets a man who only went into property development after he retired. His latest buy is a three-bed semi in West Bromwich.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Wales, a basement office in central London with a huge price tag and two flats in Bootle near Liverpool are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
